annotations.label: Logarithm (informal)

anchor.snippet: the logarithm of a number is the exponent by which another fixed value, the base

annotations.note: `Real.logb b x = Real.log x / Real.log b` directly formalizes the base-b logarithm.

annotations.label: log base 10 of 1000

anchor.snippet: the logarithm of 1000 to base 10 is 3

annotations.note: The function `Real.logb` exists but the specific numeric equality `Real.logb 10 1000 = 3` is not stated as a named lemma.

annotations.label: Common logarithm

anchor.snippet: The logarithm base 10 is called the decimal or common logarithm

annotations.note: `Real.logb 10` is an instance of the general base-b log; there is no separate named common-log constant.

annotations.label: Natural logarithm

anchor.snippet: The natural logarithm has the number e

annotations.note: `Real.log` is exactly the natural logarithm in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Binary logarithm

anchor.snippet: The binary logarithm uses base 2

annotations.note: `Nat.log2` and `Real.logb 2` provide the binary logarithm, with `Nat.log2_eq_log_two` relating them.

annotations.label: Logarithm of a product

anchor.snippet: the logarithm of a product is the sum of the logarithms of the factors

annotations.note: `Real.log_mul : log (x*y) = log x + log y` for nonzero x, y.

annotations.label: Complex logarithm

anchor.snippet: the complex logarithm is the multi-valued inverse of the complex exponential function

annotations.note: `Complex.log` is the principal-branch complex logarithm defined via `Real.log ‖x‖ + arg x * I`.

annotations.label: Discrete logarithm

anchor.snippet: the discrete logarithm is the multi-valued inverse of the exponential function in finite groups

annotations.note: No declaration named `discreteLog` was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Logarithm as inverse of exponentiation

anchor.snippet: a logarithm is the inverse operation of exponentiation

annotations.note: `Real.exp_log` (and `Real.log_exp`) express that log is the inverse of exp on positive reals.

annotations.label: Logarithm (formal definition)

anchor.snippet: the logarithm of a positive real number x with respect to base b

annotations.note: `Real.logb b x := Real.log x / Real.log b` formalizes the base-b logarithm of a positive real.

annotations.label: Logarithm notation

anchor.snippet: The logarithm is denoted

annotations.note: Mathlib uses `Real.logb` / `Real.log`; no special notation like `log_b` is introduced.

annotations.label: Logarithm as inverse function

anchor.snippet: the function log b is the inverse function to the function

annotations.note: `Real.rpow_logb` / `logb_rpow` give `b ^ logb b x = x` and conversely, expressing inversion.

annotations.label: log base 2 of 16
annotations.note: No specific lemma `Real.logb 2 16 = 4` is stated in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Negative logarithm

anchor.snippet: Logarithms can also be negative

annotations.note: `Real.log_neg : 0 < x → x < 1 → log x < 0` states that logs of numbers in (0,1) are negative.

annotations.label: log base 10 of 150

anchor.snippet: log 10 150 is approximately 2.176

annotations.note: No numerical approximation of `Real.logb 10 150` is recorded as a Mathlib lemma.

annotations.label: log_b b and log_b 1

anchor.snippet: For any base b , log b b = 1 and log b 1 = 0

annotations.note: `Real.logb_self_eq_one` (with `1 < b`) and `Real.logb_one` give the two stated identities.

annotations.label: Product, quotient, power, root identities

anchor.snippet: The logarithm of a product is the sum of the logarithms of the numbers being multiplied

annotations.note: `Real.logb_mul`, `logb_div`, `logb_pow`/`logb_rpow` cover the product, quotient, and power identities.

annotations.label: Change of base formula

anchor.snippet: The logarithm log b x can be computed from the logarithms of x and b with respect to an arbitrary base k

annotations.note: `Real.log_div_log : log x / log b = logb b x` is the change-of-base formula (with k = e).

annotations.label: Recovering the base

anchor.snippet: Given a number x and its logarithm y = log b x to an unknown base b , the base is given by

annotations.note: `Real.logb_eq_iff_rpow_eq` characterizes `logb b y = x ↔ b ^ x = y` but Mathlib has no named lemma `b = x^(1/y)`.

annotations.label: Digits of x and log10(x)

anchor.snippet: The number of digits is the smallest integer strictly bigger than log 10

annotations.note: `Nat.log` characterizes digit counts via `Nat.log_lt_of_lt_pow` etc., but the direct "number of digits = ⌊log10 x⌋ + 1" statement is not stated as such.

annotations.label: Existence of logarithm (bijection)

anchor.snippet: any continuous strictly monotonic function is bijective between its domain and range

annotations.note: Strict monotonicity plus continuity yields injection/IVT-based surjection (e.g. `StrictMono.injective`, `Continuous.surjective`) but there isn't a single named bijectivity lemma.

annotations.label: Base-b logarithm function

anchor.snippet: This function is called the base- b logarithm function or logarithmic function

annotations.note: `Real.logb` is the base-b logarithm function.

annotations.label: Characterization by product formula

anchor.snippet: the logarithm to any base b > 1 is the only increasing function f from the positive reals to the reals

annotations.note: No uniqueness characterization of log by the Cauchy-multiplicative property was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Graph of logarithm as inverse

anchor.snippet: the function log b is the inverse to the exponential function

annotations.note: `Real.rpow_logb` and `Real.logb_rpow` express that `logb b` and `b ^ ·` are inverses on positive reals.

annotations.label: Derivative of log_b x

anchor.snippet: the chain rule implies that the derivative of log b x is given by

annotations.note: `Real.deriv_log` gives d/dx log x = 1/x; a derivative lemma specifically named `deriv_logb` was not found.

annotations.label: Derivative of ln(x)

anchor.snippet: The derivative of ln( x ) is 1/ x

annotations.note: `Real.deriv_log x : deriv log x = x⁻¹`.

annotations.label: Logarithmic derivative

anchor.snippet: The quotient at the right hand side is called the logarithmic derivative of f

annotations.note: `logDeriv f` is defined as `deriv f / f` in Mathlib's calculus library.

annotations.label: Antiderivative of ln(x)

anchor.snippet: The antiderivative of the natural logarithm ln( x ) is

annotations.note: `integral_log : ∫ s in a..b, log s = b*log b - a*log a - b + a` gives the antiderivative `x log x - x`.

annotations.label: Natural logarithm as integral

anchor.section: Integral representation of the natural logarithm
anchor.snippet: The natural logarithm of t can be defined as the definite integral

annotations.note: No lemma stating `Real.log t = ∫ x in 1..t, 1/x` was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Product formula via integral

anchor.section: Integral representation of the natural logarithm
anchor.snippet: the product formula ln( tu ) = ln( t ) + ln( u ) is deduced as

annotations.note: The product formula itself is `Real.log_mul`; the integral derivation is not separately recorded.

annotations.label: Power formula via integral

anchor.section: Integral representation of the natural logarithm
anchor.snippet: The power formula ln( t r ) = r ln( t ) may be derived in a similar way

annotations.note: `Real.log_pow` and `Real.log_rpow` give `log (x^r) = r * log x`.

annotations.label: Harmonic series

anchor.section: Integral representation of the natural logarithm
anchor.snippet: is called the harmonic series

annotations.note: `harmonic n = ∑ i in range n, 1/(i+1)` is defined in `Mathlib.NumberTheory.Harmonic.Defs`.

annotations.label: Euler–Mascheroni constant

anchor.section: Integral representation of the natural logarithm
anchor.snippet: converges (i.e. gets arbitrarily close) to a number known as the Euler

annotations.note: `Real.tendsto_harmonic_sub_log` proves `harmonic n - log n → γ` (the Euler–Mascheroni constant).

annotations.label: Gelfond–Schneider theorem

anchor.snippet: The Gelfond

annotations.note: No formalization of the Gelfond–Schneider theorem was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Taylor series of ln(z)

anchor.snippet: For any real number z that satisfies 0 < z

annotations.note: `Complex.hasSum_taylorSeries_log` (and the real `Real.hasSum_pow_div_log_of_abs_lt_one`) gives the Taylor expansion of `log(1+z)`.

annotations.label: Inverse hyperbolic tangent series

anchor.snippet: Another series is based on the inverse hyperbolic tangent function

annotations.note: `Real.hasSum_log_sub_log_of_abs_lt_one` gives the arctanh-style series `log((1+x)/(1-x)) = 2∑ x^(2k+1)/(2k+1)`.

annotations.label: AGM approximation of ln

anchor.snippet: ln( x ) is approximated to a precision of 2

annotations.note: No AGM-based numerical algorithm for `log` is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Feynman's algorithm

anchor.snippet: Richard Feynman developed a bit-processing algorithm to compute the logarithm

annotations.note: Feynman's bit-processing log algorithm has no Mathlib formalization.

annotations.label: Entropy in statistical thermodynamics

anchor.snippet: the entropy S of some physical system is defined as

annotations.note: Statistical-thermodynamic entropy `S = -k ∑ p log p` is not formalized as such; only `negMulLog` exists as a building block.

annotations.label: Hausdorff dimension of Sierpinski triangle

anchor.snippet: the Hausdorff dimension of this structure ln(3)/ln(2)

annotations.note: No formalization of the Sierpinski triangle's Hausdorff dimension `log 3 / log 2` was found.

annotations.label: Prime number theorem

anchor.snippet: The prime number theorem asserts that

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `Nat.primeCounting` but does not contain a proof of the prime number theorem asymptotic π(x) ~ x/ln x.

annotations.label: Offset logarithmic integral

anchor.snippet: the offset logarithmic integral function Li( x )

annotations.note: No `logIntegral` or offset Li function is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Log of n factorial

anchor.snippet: The logarithm of n factorial

annotations.note: Stirling-style bounds on `log n!` (e.g. `le_log_factorial_stirling`) are in Mathlib, but the asymptotic `log n! ~ n log n - n` is not stated as such.

annotations.label: Complex logarithm (formal)

anchor.snippet: are called complex logarithms of z

annotations.note: `Complex.log z = Real.log ‖z‖ + arg z * I` is the (principal-branch) complex log.

annotations.label: Principal argument

anchor.snippet: the so-called principal argument

annotations.note: `Complex.arg z ∈ (-π, π]` is the principal argument (`Complex.arg_mem_Ioc`).

annotations.label: Euler's formula

anchor.snippet: Euler's formula connects the trigonometric functions sine and cosine to the complex exponential

annotations.note: `Complex.exp_mul_I : exp (θ * I) = cos θ + sin θ * I` is Euler's formula.

anchors.snippet: Euler's formula connects the trigonometric functions sine and cosine to the complex exponential

annotations.label: Principal value of complex logarithm

anchor.snippet: a k is called the principal value of the logarithm

annotations.note: `Complex.log` is defined as the principal value via `Real.log ‖x‖ + arg x * I` with `arg ∈ (-π, π]`.

annotations.label: Matrix logarithm

anchor.snippet: the logarithm of a matrix is the (multi-valued) inverse function of the matrix exponential

annotations.note: Mathlib has `Matrix.exp` (NormedSpace.exp) but no formalization of a matrix logarithm.

annotations.label: p-adic logarithm

anchor.snippet: the p -adic logarithm , the inverse function of the p -adic exponential

annotations.note: No p-adic logarithm or p-adic exponential is defined in Mathlib's Padics.

annotations.label: Discrete logarithm (formal)

anchor.snippet: The discrete logarithm is the integer n solving the equation

annotations.note: No `discreteLog`/`DiscreteLog` declaration was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Zech's logarithm

anchor.snippet: Zech's logarithm is related to the discrete logarithm

annotations.note: No formalization of Zech's logarithm was found.

annotations.label: Group isomorphism via log

anchor.snippet: the identity log( cd ) = log( c ) + log( d ) expresses a group isomorphism

annotations.note: `Real.log_mul` is the homomorphism identity, but Mathlib does not package log as a named `MulHom`/`MonoidHom` between (ℝ>0,·) and (ℝ,+).

annotations.label: Polylogarithm

anchor.snippet: The polylogarithm is the function defined by

annotations.note: No polylogarithm function `Li_s(z) = ∑ z^k/k^s` is defined in Mathlib.
